claims.claim: The 48D codec lane maps character rhythm, word certainty, emotional markers, projected embeddings, and narrative arc into Astrid's inner speech surface.
High entropy can make tail vibrancy feel wider than a static ceiling; the system should preserve bounded evidence about clipping and tail-only lifts.
Narrative arc should be treated as a path/trajectory rather than only a single slope.

Changing live codec gain, feature ceilings, semantic dimensions, or live narrative-vector shape would alter live sensory/control behavior and requires Mike/operator approval with a V2 authority boundary.
